title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Relational Lens: Capstone Defense
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql capstone defense increment in a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.
Define correctness, latency, saturation, lock, plan-regression, backup, and data-quality signals with response thresholds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Capstone Defense · Theory Capstone Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 theory artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 2; justify any non-relational component.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Capstone Defense contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Capstone Defense · Theory Capstone Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence. For Predict Capstone Defense · Theory Capstone Defense, keep Capstone Defense connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 worked example artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 2; justify any non-relational component. A defensible solution applies Capstone Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio.

content.code:
SELECT ticket_id, status, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Record SQLite behavior, then verify syntax and semantics against PostgreSQL 18.4.
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Passing the original demo proves the database system is production ready. This fails the Capstone Defense contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Capstone Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Passing the original demo proves the database system is production ready.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the capstone defense defect evidence boundary.
Run a convenient Capstone Defense query for repair the capstone defense defect, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.
Reconstruct the conditions for Capstone Defense, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Build Capstone Defense evidence · Theory Capstone Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-1lm80d" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; dialect, storage-model, distribution, or production evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Capstone Defense evidence · Theory Capstone Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capstone Defense into observable performance evidence. For Build Capstone Defense evidence · Theory Capstone Defense, keep Capstone Defense connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-1lm80d
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT typeof(owner_id) AS sqlite_runtime_type, COUNT(*) AS rows
FROM tickets GROUP BY typeof(owner_id) ORDER BY sqlite_runtime_type;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.
